title: sideritis arborescens subsp. luteola
intro: Sideritis arborescens subsp. luteola, also known as Ironwort or Greek Mountain Tea, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. This herb has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sideritis arborescens subsp. luteola include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
